action.hypothesis: Nine no-improve rounds indicate the current search direction around lowrank_gated_residual is exhausted, and the latest architecture pivot to `jumprelu` remained clearly uncompetitive even under the strongest shared recipe. The least-bad next informative move is to switch the single primary variable to a different existing architecture mechanism with real prior signal: rerun `two_stage_residual` at K=128 under the current strongest stable recipe (`EXPANSION_FACTOR=32`, signum, `LR=1.6e-3`, `AUXK_ALPHA=0`) to test whether hierarchical residual allocation benefits from the stronger modern training regime.
action.summary: Run a proxy comparison with `ARCHITECTURE=two_stage_residual` at K=128 using the current strong shared training recipe, changing only architecture. This is a fundamentally different sparse-mechanism probe after both lowrank_gated_residual tuning and the refreshed `jumprelu` comparison failed to improve the frontier.
action.self_review: This round follows the mandatory architecture pivot and avoids another minor lowrank_gated_residual tweak after repeated stagnation. It also stays on an already implemented, previously working family with some real full-signal evidence, which is lower risk than opening a brand-new code path immediately after multiple failed directions.
notes_to_memory: After the refreshed `jumprelu` probe stayed far behind, the next architecture pivot moves to `two_stage_residual` rather than returning to lowrank_gated_residual micro-tuning.

`two_stage_residual` is chosen because it is a genuinely different hierarchical sparse-allocation mechanism with prior working evidence, but it has not yet been re-tested under the strongest current shared recipe.
This round isolates architecture only and checks whether residual reconstruction staged across two passes can recover quality that single-pass alternatives are missing at the K=128 anchor.
next_hypotheses: If `two_stage_residual` is competitive at K=128 proxy, promote it to full before any family-local tuning.
If `two_stage_residual` remains clearly behind, archive it as a current mainline challenger and plan the next round around a new architecture mechanism rather than more local sweeps.
If `two_stage_residual` shows promising quality with poor throughput, follow with a narrow performance-oriented code fix instead of discarding the family on architecture grounds.
